questions.question: Why are the Himalayas important for India?
questions.answer: The Himalayas protect India from cold winds and act as the source of many major rivers.

questions.question: What are the main characteristics of the Himadri range?
questions.answer: The Himadri is the highest Himalayan range with an average height of about 6000 metres and contains the highest peaks.

questions.question: Name two valleys found in the Himachal range.
questions.answer: Kullu Valley and Kangra Valley.

questions.question: What are Duns? Give one example.
questions.answer: Duns are valleys between the Lesser Himalayas and Shiwaliks. Example: Dehra Dun.

questions.question: Why are the Northern Plains important for agriculture?
questions.answer: Because they have fertile alluvial soil, adequate water supply and flat land suitable for farming.

questions.question: What is meant by Bhangar?
questions.answer: Bhangar is the older alluvial soil found above the flood plains of rivers.

questions.question: What is Khadar?
questions.answer: Khadar is the newer alluvial soil deposited by rivers every year in flood plains.

questions.question: Why is Majuli island famous?
questions.answer: Majuli is the largest inhabited river island in the world located in the Brahmaputra river.

questions.question: What are the Central Highlands?
questions.answer: The Central Highlands are the northern part of the Peninsular Plateau lying north of the Narmada River.

questions.question: Which rivers flow through the Central Highlands?
questions.answer: Chambal, Sind, Betwa and Ken.

questions.question: Why is the Peninsular Plateau rich in minerals?
questions.answer: Because it is composed of ancient crystalline rocks containing mineral deposits.

questions.question: What is the Deccan Plateau?
questions.answer: The Deccan Plateau is a triangular landmass located south of the Narmada River.

questions.question: Name the three hill ranges of the Meghalaya Plateau.
questions.answer: Garo Hills, Khasi Hills and Jaintia Hills.

questions.question: Why are Western Ghats important?
questions.answer: They cause orographic rainfall and influence the climate of the region.

questions.question: What are the features of the Indian Desert?
questions.answer: It has sandy plains, sand dunes, low rainfall and sparse vegetation.

questions.question: What are barchans?
questions.answer: Barchans are crescent-shaped sand dunes found in deserts.

questions.question: What is the Konkan coast?
questions.answer: The Konkan coast is the northern part of the western coastal plains between Mumbai and Goa.

questions.question: What is the Malabar coast?
questions.answer: The Malabar coast is the southern part of the western coastal plains located in Kerala.

questions.question: What is the Coromandel Coast?
questions.answer: The Coromandel Coast is the southern part of the eastern coastal plains.

questions.question: Name four rivers that form large deltas in the eastern coast.
questions.answer: Mahanadi, Godavari, Krishna and Kaveri.

questions.question: Why are coastal plains important?
questions.answer: They support fishing, agriculture and port activities.

questions.question: What are coral islands?
questions.answer: Coral islands are islands formed by coral deposits in the sea.

questions.question: Where are Lakshadweep islands located?
questions.answer: They are located in the Arabian Sea near the coast of Kerala.

questions.question: Where are Andaman and Nicobar Islands located?
questions.answer: They are located in the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Why are India's island groups strategically important?
questions.answer: Because they are located near important sea routes and help in national security.
